dropPdf: Drop a PDF file here or click to upload
invalidRange: Page range must be between 1 and {max}
overlapRange: Page ranges overlap — pages in multiple ranges will appear in each output

corruptedPdf: This PDF file is corrupted and cannot be read

emptyGroup: Click pages above to add them to this group

descriptions.title: About PDF Splitter
descriptions.aeoDefinition: PDF Splitter is a free online tool for splitting PDF files into separate pages, custom page ranges, or hand-picked selections. Preview thumbnails, download individually or as ZIP. All processing runs locally in your browser.
descriptions.whatIs: Split a PDF file into separate pages or custom page ranges directly in your browser. Upload your PDF, choose a split mode (extract every page, split by ranges, or select specific pages), preview thumbnails, and download the results. No data is uploaded to any server — all processing uses the pdf-lib library.
descriptions.useCasesP1: Extract specific pages from a long report or contract to share only the relevant sections.
descriptions.useCasesP2: Split a multi-page PDF into individual pages for separate processing or distribution.
descriptions.step1Text: Drag and drop your PDF file or click to select it from your computer.
descriptions.step2Text: Select how to split: extract every page, define custom ranges, or click thumbnails to pick specific pages.
descriptions.step3Text: Click the Split button to generate the output files. Download individually or as a ZIP archive.
descriptions.faq1Q: Is my PDF data sent to a server?
descriptions.faq1A: No. All processing happens entirely in your browser. Your PDF files never leave your device.
descriptions.faq2Q: What PDF features are preserved after splitting?
descriptions.faq2A: Text, images, fonts, annotations, and form fields are preserved. Some advanced features like JavaScript actions may not carry over.
descriptions.faq3Q: Is there a file size limit?
descriptions.faq3A: There is no strict limit, but very large files (hundreds of pages) may take longer to process. The tool handles files of any size your browser can hold in memory.
